query: variant(variantId: "17-43057062-T-TG", dataset: gnomad_r4) — BRCA1 c.5266dupC in the current GRCh38 release
description: BRCA1 c.5266dupC viewed through gnomAD r4 — the canonical Ashkenazi-founder frameshift duplication. The fixture captures the textbook population-genetics signal: global AF is ~7e-5 (rare overall), but ASJ exome AF is ~1.2e-3, a ~17x enrichment over global. This is the kind of per-population context that distinguishes gnomAD from dbSNP's aggregated MAFs and is the reason lookup_gnomad exists as a separate tool. variant_id is in gnomAD's left-aligned VCF anchor-base form (17-43057062-T-TG), one base 5' of the HGVS right-shifted position (43057063) and using T as the anchor base for the +G insertion.
fetched_via: POST https://gnomad.broadinstitute.org/api with GraphQL query for variant{variant_id reference_genome chrom pos ref alt rsids flags exome{ac an af filters populations{id ac an}} genome{ac an af filters populations{id ac an}}}
